# smc_sub_href (Data Source)

Enables the retrieval of the "href" (aka "id") of a nested resources.

For example, it can be used to obtain the "href" of sub-resources that
are implicitly created along with a firewall, such as internal
gateways or endpoints.

```hcl
data "smc_sub_href" "ep_192_168_100_14" {
  	from_ref = "http://mysmc:8082/7.4/elements/single_fw/268587556/internal_gateway"
  	match = "mygateway/internal_endpoint/192.168.100.14"
}
```
  then you would use it this way:

```hcl
locals {
     # the value of my_endpoint_id would be something like:
     # http://localhost:8082/7.4/elements/single_fw/12345/internal_gateway/6789/internal_endpoint/101112
     my_endpoint_id = data.smc_sub_href.ep_192_168_100_14.id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `match` (String) An expression to locate the desired sub-element.
e.g. to get and endpoint from firewall reference, you need a match expression like this:

```
match="mygateway/internal_endpoint/192.168.100.14"
```

### Optional

- `from_ref` (String) The url from where to search for sub-element.

e.g. to start searching for the href of an internal gateway or its
sub-elements of a given firewall, use:

	from_ref = "http://mysmc:8082/7.4/elements/single_fw/268587556/internal_gateway"

Note that you should not have raw urls like this in your terraform
file: The url of a resource such as a single firewall is accessible
via the terraform "id" attribute or via its "lk" attribute, example:

    from_ref = "${smc_single_fw.my_single_fw.id}/internal_gateway"

could also be written:

    from_ref = smc_single_fw.my_single_fw.lk.internal_gateway

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) returns the href of the sub-resource matching query
